fault_hypothesis: The `overlaps` inner function uses strict inequality (`b[1] <= a[0] or b[0] >= a[1]`) to detect non-overlap, which means touching boundaries (where b[1]==a[0] or b[0]==a[1]) are incorrectly treated as non-overlapping. The bug is on line 62 where the overlap condition should use `<` instead of `<=` and `>` instead of `>=`.

spec.expr: not (candidate[1] == slot[0] or candidate[0] == slot[1]) or overlaps(slot, candidate)

constraints.intent: When candidate touches an existing slot at a boundary, overlaps must return True (touching should be treated as overlapping).
After each loop iteration, if candidate touches slot at a boundary, the function should have already returned False (not reached loop tail).
If overlaps returned False (else branch taken), candidate must be strictly non-adjacent to slot — touching boundaries should have triggered the then-branch.

spec.expr: __cbfl_result[0] is False or all(r[1] < candidate[0] or r[0] > candidate[1] for r in existing)

constraints.intent: If accepted is True, candidate must not touch or overlap any existing slot.
